summary: The proposed delta is directionally faithful: it makes quick registered-headless-only, rejects native/inline/unknown quick transports, preserves direct inline and standard+ fallback behavior, adopts qualified dispatch-depth fields, and excludes composition. It is not ready for the atomic PRD transaction because the normative terminology omits Codex native subagents, dispatch_depth is defined in a way that conflicts with direct and fallback execution, quick node/session/attempt cardinality can accidentally override retained v19 retry semantics, and the transport vocabulary remains internally ambiguous.

findings.title: The execution-surface terminology contract omits Codex native subagents
findings.evidence: SD-75 normatively defines Claude subagent, Claude agent-team teammate session, and registered headless worker session, but it does not define Codex native subagent. SD-74 mentions Codex agents.max_depth only as a setting boundary. The assigned decision requires four explicit surfaces: Codex native subagents, Claude subagents, Claude agent-team teammate sessions, and registered headless worker sessions.
findings.impact: A projection can satisfy SD-75 while still using an ambiguous generic subagent label for Codex, so the requested surface distinction is not fully enforceable.
findings.required_change: Add a normative SD-75 row for 'Codex native subagent': a runtime-native Codex child thread/session governed by Codex native subagent configuration such as agents.max_depth; it is not a registered headless worker and its evidence cannot satisfy headless eligibility. Keep 'runtime-native subagent' as the cross-runtime category for Codex native subagents and Claude subagents only; explicitly exclude Claude agent-team teammates.

findings.title: dispatch_depth is conflated with registration even though direct and fallback execution are not registered
findings.evidence: SD-74.2 calls dispatch_depth a 'portable registered-dispatch ownership level', but the proposal also assigns dispatch_depth=0 to unregistered direct inline execution and allows a standard+ route node to retain dispatch_depth while the actual attempt executes as a native subagent or inline fallback. SD-74.4 recognizes the node/attempt difference but does not resolve the contradictory definition.
findings.impact: Implementers could either erase logical node depth during fallback or falsely describe native/inline attempts as registered workers, breaking Fleet and eligibility evidence semantics.
findings.required_change: Define dispatch_depth as the portable logical route-ownership/topology level, independent of runtime transport, process ancestry, runtime-native nesting, and registration. Define attempt execution surface separately. State that only an attempt launched through the repo registration contract is a registered headless worker; direct inline and native/inline fallback attempts retain their route node's dispatch_depth metadata without acquiring registered-worker status. Require node and attempt fields to be unambiguous in route, registry, completion, and Fleet evidence.

findings.title: Quick node/session/attempt cardinality is ambiguous and can override retained v19 retry policy
findings.evidence: The proposal requires exactly one quick capability-owner worker session and criterion 11 says exactly one canonical attempt row. V19 separately permits a serial capacity failover attempt for the same route node and preserves distinct exact attempt rows. The approved v20 decision forbids native/inline substitution; it does not approve removal of headless-only retry/failover behavior.
findings.impact: The transaction could unintentionally repeal SD-59 or make Fleet evidence contradictory whenever a registered headless quick attempt terminates and a permitted registered headless retry occurs.
findings.required_change: Specify exactly one quick route node, no child nodes, and at most one live attempt/session at a time. Preserve existing serial retry/capacity rules unless separately superseded, but require every retry to remain registered headless and to have its own canonical terminal attempt row. Reword Fleet acceptance to require exactly one current/open quick attempt row, while historical terminal rows remain valid. If all eligible registered-headless attempts fail, terminate quick with a structured failure; native-subagent and inline attempt counts remain zero.

findings.title: The closed transport vocabulary is not defined consistently with SD-32 and SD-72
findings.evidence: V19 SD-32 lists headless, native-subagent, detached-process, and inline-fallback as transport values, while SD-72 says the canonical dispatch-tuple transport is headless|interactive. The proposed delta calls native-subagent, inline-fallback, and interactive transports and demands a closed vocabulary without declaring whether these are one enum or separate launch-transport, execution-surface, and fallback-hop namespaces.
findings.impact: A compiler can reject arbitrary strings yet still disagree across adapters about which known values are globally valid, recipe-valid, or merely fallback labels.
findings.required_change: Add an explicit closed schema for each namespace. Keep the registered wrapper transport machine value headless. Identify native-subagent and inline-fallback as execution-surface/fallback choices if they are not canonical dispatch-tuple transports, and define where interactive and detached-process are valid. Apply recipe allowlists after global vocabulary validation. Quick must allow only the registered headless surface; explicit empty, interactive, native-subagent, inline-fallback, and arbitrary values must fail before route emission.

findings.title: Fail-closed error and recovery semantics are suggestive rather than normative
findings.evidence: SD-73 says the structured error may be 'such as quick-headless-unavailable', while the acceptance criterion expects that exact value. The risk section says recovery requires a new route/intensity decision, but the normative decision does not state that recovery boundary or distinguish compile-time ineligibility from post-compile headless failure.
findings.impact: Adapters can emit incompatible errors or silently recompile at another intensity while still claiming conceptual compliance.
findings.required_change: Make quick-headless-unavailable the exact compile-time error for checked headless ineligibility, with route file, registry claim, and child spawn all absent. Define the post-compile terminal error for exhausted registered-headless attempts. State normatively that recovery requires owner-authorized route recompilation at a different intensity; the compiler must not mutate intensity, transport, or execution location in place.

findings.title: The migration rule needs a deterministic boundary for normative prose and legacy artifacts
findings.evidence: The proposal permits legacy read-only compatibility and acknowledges extensive historical bare-depth prose, but it does not define how a conformance check distinguishes retained historical quotation/history from current normative text or prevents legacy records from being resumed and re-emitted.
findings.impact: The PRD or generated projections can retain ambiguous current language while tests only inspect newly emitted JSON fields.
findings.required_change: Require zero ambiguous bare depth/max_depth/owner_depth in current normative prose, schemas, and generated portable/adapter projections. Permit old terms only inside explicitly labeled historical/legacy blocks or versioned read-only records. Require legacy readers to tag the source schema version and prohibit resume/re-emit until recompilation produces qualified v20 fields.

required_changes: Add Codex native subagent to SD-75 and define the four required execution surfaces explicitly.
Redefine dispatch_depth as logical portable route ownership, then separate route-node depth from actual attempt execution surface and registered-worker status.
Clarify quick cardinality as one node and at most one live registered-headless attempt, preserving any existing serial headless-only retry semantics and forbidding native/inline attempts.
Define closed, non-overlapping vocabularies for dispatch transport, execution surface, and fallback hop; apply quick's headless-only allowlist after global validation.
Make fail-closed error enums and owner-authorized recovery behavior normative for compile-time and post-compile failures.
Set an explicit current-versus-legacy terminology migration boundary and conformance gate.
Keep SD-19 visibly superseded by SD-73; preserve its text only as labeled history, never as a current fallback rule.
Do not add composition schema, co-primary routing, cross-capability DAGs, source implementation details, concrete model selection, or runtime configuration changes.

approved_decision.quick_registered_headless_only: covered but blocked by findings V20-R3 and V20-R4
approved_decision.quick_unknown_transport_fail_closed: covered but vocabulary must be normalized by V20-R4
approved_decision.standard_plus_fallback_preserved: covered; must remain explicitly scoped to standard+ and must not be weakened by v20
approved_decision.dispatch_depth_terminology: covered directionally but definition must be corrected by V20-R2
approved_decision.four_surface_distinction: not fully covered; Codex native subagent is missing from SD-75

acceptance_criteria.note: Also require the sole quick node's dispatch_depth=1 explicitly.
Distinguish omitted transport, which derives headless, from explicit empty, which fails.
Make quick-headless-unavailable exact and add post-compile exhausted-headless behavior.
Closed vocabularies/namespaces are not yet defined consistently.
Direct remains main-inline at dispatch_depth=0 with no worker row.
Preserves same-harness headless, cross-harness headless, native-subagent, then inline for standard+.
One quick node; no child or dispatch_depth=2 fan-out.
Codex agents.max_depth and native checks cannot establish registered-headless eligibility.
Claude distinction is correct, but the complete four-surface contract requires a Codex native-subagent term too.
Exactly one row must mean one current/open attempt, not one lifetime row, unless retry repeal is separately approved.
Require explicit version tagging and no resume/re-emission before v20 recompilation.
Composition and co-primary behavior are excluded.
Parity requirement is specification-level only and correctly requires independent adapter enforcement.

scope_rejections: multi-capability composition or co-primary route design
source/compiler/registry/Fleet implementation detail beyond testable acceptance behavior

atomic_prd_transaction_guidance.supersession: SD-73 explicitly supersedes SD-19. SD-19 may remain only as labeled reversal history.

minimum_normative_model.quick: one capability-owner route node at owner_dispatch_depth=1 and dispatch_depth=1; max_dispatch_depth=1; allowed execution surface is registered headless only; no children; no native or inline fallback
minimum_normative_model.direct: main-inline at dispatch_depth=0; no registered worker row
minimum_normative_model.standard_plus: existing capability recipe and checked same-harness headless -> cross-harness headless -> native-subagent -> inline fallback order retained
minimum_normative_model.depth: logical portable route ownership; independent of runtime-native nesting and actual fallback execution surface

atomic_prd_transaction_guidance.transaction_gate: Apply all required changes in one locked PRD/state/summary/version transaction. Do not proceed from the research wording unchanged.

qa_assurance.performed: One separate registered-headless Codex deep-review worker inspected only the assigned PRD, audit, research shard, and route record.
qa_assurance.fact_boundary: Vendor-runtime facts were accepted only as recorded in the assigned audit; this stage did not independently browse or re-probe vendor documentation.
qa_assurance.read_marker_warning: The required PRD read-marker command was attempted, but the runtime could not write under /home/Uihyeop/agent_setting/.spec-grounding because it was read-only. The route record already carries prevalidated spec_read evidence.
